title: What My Sister Buried
theme: Silence is just fear wearing cashmere.

cast.name: Gavin Bennett
traits: replies to texts while processing betrayal
owns a structured coat for crisis scenes
secret_capacity: buried one traumatic detail under work for years
cast.regional_responsibility: has keys to a church annex

cast.name: Wyatt Pike
traits: briefly leaves the room after difficult questions
secret_capacity: deleted a voicemail and regretted it spiritually
has a storage unit he refuses to explain
cast.regional_responsibility: organizes a fundraiser with tense undertones

cast.name: Sienna Hawthorne
traits: appears exactly when gossip is needed
says the dangerous thing out loud
secret_capacity: once hid a casserole dish containing evidence
may die first or survive unbelievably
cast.regional_responsibility: is trusted to lock up after emotionally charged events
cast.suspicion_profile: appears trustworthy under pendant lighting only

cast.name: Mara Dane
traits: can be therapist, realtor, widow, murderer, or all simultaneously
secret_capacity: had brunch with someone officially deceased
knows why the old orchard chapel was closed
cast.regional_responsibility: organizes a fundraiser with tense undertones
cast.suspicion_profile: appears trustworthy under pendant lighting only

cast.name: Noah Winterson
secret_capacity: deleted a voicemail and regretted it spiritually
has a storage unit he refuses to explain
cast.regional_responsibility: is trusted to lock up after emotionally charged events
cast.suspicion_profile: can host Thanksgiving but could move a body-shaped rug

cast.name: Mara Bennett
secret_capacity: accused an innocent scamp and feels sick about it
cast.regional_responsibility: is trusted to lock up after emotionally charged events
cast.suspicion_profile: appears trustworthy under pendant lighting only

cast.name: Brooke Pike
traits: notices timeline errors before adults do

cast.name: Elias Cross
traits: knows the old case too well

beat_sheet.text: Gavin Bennett relocates to Blackthorn Lake after an emotionally negligent implosion and pretends work explains everything. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.00.

Brooke Pike hates the move and immediately distrusts Wyatt Pike, a financial advisor with suspiciously healthy handyman energy. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.07. A phone vibrates from inside the wall.

A wine scene at The Candle Finch introduces the snow globe and local gossip about the vineyard fire. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.14.

Mara Dane reveals a perfectly phrased warning in a well-lit kitchen while bees in winter. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.23. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

A cliffside emotional exposition reframes overbearing mother tension as a civic hazard. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.31.

Gavin Bennett snoops through a laptop, finds nothing conclusive, and becomes more certain anyway. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.40.

The town offers a fake sense of safety through a fundraiser, a casserole, and a sheriff who suspects the wrong person first. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.50. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

Wyatt Pike is accused, gaslighted, then gently framed near hydrangeas until the blood-adjacent tea towel turns up somewhere impossible. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.61.

A rain argument escalates into basement discourse and the fake moral theme: 'A town never forgets. It just redecorates.' Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.72. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

Someone goes missing after mentioning the unopened letter and the old orchard chapel in the same sentence. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.84.

The basement reveal ties ceramic owl to Blackthorn Vineyard, St. Agnes Regional, and a brunch resurrection event. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.97.

Brooke Pike spots the timeline error that every adult has spiritually rented around. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.10. Someone who should be dead appears at brunch.

A frantic drive toward the gazebo at Lake Mercy ends with ambulance lights and a one-line empowerment speech. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.25.

Mara Dane confesses just enough to make the truth worse while the sheriff arrives exactly fourteen minutes too late. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.41. A basement light switches on in an empty house.

Sequel bait lands when the ceramic owl reappears and someone whispers about the Winterson case. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.58. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

contradiction_ledger.entry: Wyatt Pike is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Gavin Bennett says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Alder Ridge.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Wyatt?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Brooke. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Gavin. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The snow globe is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Mara."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
fake_critic_blurb: "What My Sister Buried understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "What My Sister Buried" follows Gavin Bennett, an overextended professional woman who mistakes productivity for safety until overbearing mother tension collides with Wyatt Pike, a comforting regional adult carrying portable suspicion. As bees in winter, Blackthorn County reveals once again that every secret comes home. Every town has a basement.
